document.title: F21A NR Clinical Evaluation Assessment Report No. MDR027_2025
document.revisionOrVersion: F21A NR Rev. 4; Clinical Evaluation Report A-CER-HNX Rev.4 referenced in final assessment

sourceSummary.oneLine: Notified Body 2265 clinical evaluation assessment for H&abyz HnX-P1 and HnX-PB Portable X-ray System under EU MDR.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports conformity assessment review of clinical evaluation, clinical evidence, PMS/PMCF, IFU alignment, equivalence, and benefit-risk conclusions for CE MDR certification.
keyPoints: The device is a Class IIb mobile radiographic unit classified under Rule 10.
The final NB2265 assessment accepted the manufacturer's response and rationale.
The device was considered safe and effective with regard to the defined intended use.
Clinical evaluation relied on literature, equivalent device data, and non-clinical technical data rather than manufacturer-conducted clinical investigations.
Equivalence was demonstrated to FDR_Xair (XD2000) manufactured by Fujifilm.

concepts.name: EU MDR conformity assessment
concepts.evidence: Chosen conformity assessment process as per Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex IX

concepts.name: Class IIb Rule 10 classification
concepts.evidence: Device class / classification rule IIb / Rule 10

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The HnX-P1 (and HnX-PB) is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.

concepts.name: Indications for use
concepts.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System indications for use
concepts.evidence: The device is indicated for the diagnostic imaging of the following clinical conditions: Traumatic and Orthopedic Conditions; Respiratory and Thoracic Conditions; Abdominal Conditions.

concepts.name: Contraindications
concepts.evidence: Pregnancy: The device is contraindicated for use in the abdominal and pelvic regions of women who are pregnant or may be pregnant.

concepts.name: Undesirable side-effects
concepts.evidence: Undesirable side-effects have been updated to include cataract risk and characterized in accordance with MDCG 2023-3 Rev.2 Point 10

concepts.name: Equivalence under MDR Annex XIV
concepts.pageTarget: Clinical evaluation equivalence under MDR Annex XIV
concepts.evidence: The devices are equivalent in all three aspects – biological, technical and clinical.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer properly planned and addressed PMS and PMCF.

concepts.name: Summary of safety and clinical performance applicability
concepts.evidence: The requirement of Article 32 of the MDR is not applicable, it is not a Class III or implantable medical device.

evidence.claim: The assessment concerns a Portable X-ray System with models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical device/ trade name Portable X-ray System, Models: HnX-P1, HnX-PB

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co. Ltd. with SRN KR-MF-000020684.
evidence.evidenceText: Name and address of the manufacturer and SRN H&abyz Co. Ltd., 2-dog, 41-16 Cheoinseong-Ro, Namsa-Eup, Cheoin-Gu, Yongin-Si, Gyeonggi-Do, 17118, Republic of Korea, SRN: KR-MF-000020684

evidence.claim: The authorised representative is Javitech Consulting GmbH. with SRN DE-AR-000005875.
evidence.evidenceText: Name and Address of the Authorised Representative and SRN Javitech Consulting GmbH., Sachsenhausener Str.16, 65824 Schwalbach am Taunus, Germany DE-AR-000005875

evidence.claim: The notified body is 3EC International a.s., Notified Body No. 2265.
evidence.evidenceText: Notified body No. 2265 3EC International a.s., Hraničná 18, 821 05 Bratislava

evidence.claim: The device is a mobile radiographic unit.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical device type Mobile radiographic units

evidence.claim: The Basic UDI-DI listed for the device is 88000704HNXWP.
evidence.evidenceText: Basic UDI-DI(s) (if available) 88000704HNXWP

evidence.claim: The device is classified as Class IIb under Rule 10.
evidence.evidenceText: Device class / classification rule IIb / Rule 10

evidence.claim: The assessment process chosen was Annex IX under Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: Chosen conformity assessment process as per Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex IX

evidence.claim: The final NB2265 review was closed on 05.03.2026.
evidence.evidenceText: Final NB2265 review 25.02.2026 / 05.03.2026 closed

evidence.claim: The final intended purpose states that HnX-P1 and HnX-PB generate radiographic images of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 (and HnX-PB) is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.
evidence.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System intended purpose

evidence.claim: The intended patient population is adult patients.
evidence.evidenceText: Age: Adult patients.
evidence.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System target population

evidence.claim: The device indications include traumatic and orthopedic conditions, respiratory and thoracic conditions, and abdominal conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is indicated for the diagnostic imaging of the following clinical conditions: Traumatic and Orthopedic Conditions; Respiratory and Thoracic Conditions; Abdominal Conditions.
evidence.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System indications for use

evidence.claim: Final indication wording included suspected abdominal conditions such as bowel obstruction and pneumoperitoneum.
evidence.evidenceText: Abdominal Conditions: Suspected abdominal conditions (e.g., bowel obstruction, pneumoperitoneum).
evidence.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System abdominal indications

evidence.claim: Use in abdominal and pelvic regions of pregnant or potentially pregnant women is contraindicated.
evidence.evidenceText: Pregnancy: The device is contraindicated for use in the abdominal and pelvic regions of women who are pregnant or may be pregnant.

evidence.claim: The device is contraindicated for non-extremity examinations in bariatric patients with BMI 40 kg/m^2.
evidence.evidenceText: Bariatric Patients: The device is strictly contraindicated for any examination other than the extremities (i.e., limb imaging) in bariatric patients (defined as BMI 40 kg/m^2).

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Mammography: This device is not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The claimed lifetime of the device is 7 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Information regarding the product's lifetime (7 years) has been added.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer did not conduct clinical investigations for the device under evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Has the manufacturer conducted clinical investigation(s) for the device under evaluation? No
evidence.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System clinical investigations

evidence.claim: Clinical safety and performance were supported by sufficient clinical data from literature and equivalent devices, plus non-clinical technical data.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer has demonstrated the device's clinical safety and performance by the evaluation of Sufficient Clinical Data derived from the literature and equivalent devices, coupled with extensive Non-Clinical (Technical) Data.
evidence.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System clinical evidence

evidence.claim: The manufacturer demonstrated equivalence to FDR_Xair (XD2000) manufactured by Fujifilm.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer has demonstrated equivalence with respect to section 3 of Annex XIV of the MDR to FDR_Xair (XD2000) portable X-ray medical device manufactured by Fujifilm.

evidence.claim: The equivalent device was considered equivalent in biological, technical and clinical aspects.
evidence.evidenceText: The devices are equivalent in all three aspects – biological, technical and clinical.
evidence.pageTarget: Clinical evaluation equivalence under MDR Annex XIV

evidence.claim: PMCF was planned.
evidence.evidenceText: Is there a post-market clinical follow-up planned? Yes - Accepted

evidence.claim: The PMS and PMCF planning were judged adequate in the assessment outcome.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer properly planned and addressed PMS and PMCF.
evidence.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System PMS and PMCF

evidence.claim: The SSCP requirement under MDR Article 32 was considered not applicable because the device is not Class III or implantable.
evidence.evidenceText: The requirement of Article 32 of the MDR is not applicable, it is not a Class III or implantable medical device.

evidence.claim: The final assessment concluded the device can be considered safe and effective for the defined intended use.
evidence.evidenceText: On the basis of the manufacturer’s Clinical Evaluation Report, clinical evidence and relevant parts of the technical documentation I declare that assessed Medical device: - can be considered as safe and effective with regard to the defined intended use.

evidence.claim: The final clinical expert opinion recommended continuing in the conformity assessment process.
evidence.evidenceText: On the basis of the clinical evaluation and clinical evidence assessment results, the Internalclinician: recommends to continue in the conformity assessment process.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document content is an EU MDR Notified Body 2265 assessment by 3EC International a.s.
Document header contains F21A NR Rev. 4 dated 06.05.2024, while final assessment evidence references Clinical Evaluation Report A-CER-HNX Rev.4 dated 03032026 and closure on 05.03.2026.
Several template sections contain unresolved Yes / No placeholders, but final outcome sections provide accepted conclusions.
OCR/text quality includes typographical errors and inconsistent wording such as 'manufafcturer', 'exective', and 'Internalclinician'.
